summary: Pipe vs ZMQ environment IPC comparison

pipe.note: Each reset kills + restarts ns-3 process

zmq.note: Process stays alive across episodes; Simulator::Destroy + rebuild per reset

analysis.bottleneck: ns-3 simulation steps (~18-20s each) dominate total time, not IPC or reset

recommendation: Use Pipe env for simplicity. Reset overhead is negligible (<0.5% of episode time). ZMQ adds complexity (extra binary, port management, process lifecycle) for minimal speedup. ZMQ becomes beneficial only if step duration is reduced (e.g., shorter stepDuration or faster ns-3 execution).
